summary: Cantrill announces that Devon O'Dell has begun serious work on porting DTrace to FreeBSD, enabled by OpenSolaris's CDDL license which permits component ports to other systems. While others had previously talked about such a port, O'Dell is the first to actually begin the work. Cantrill acknowledges the complexity of the task given DTrace's Solaris-specific dependencies but expresses confidence it can be done with community collaboration.
key_points: The CDDL license enables porting OpenSolaris components like DTrace to other operating systems
Devon O'Dell is the first person to seriously begin work on a FreeBSD port of DTrace
DTrace has complex dependencies on Solaris-specific components that make porting non-trivial
Team DTrace is offering support and collaboration to anyone working on the port
money_quotes: One of the exciting things about OpenSolaris is that it's released under a license – the CDDL – that allows ports of individual components to other systems.
This has been talked about before (in some cases, with braggadocio), but Devon is the first to start the work in earnest.
And indeed, work it will be: DTrace isn't a simple system, and it has several dependencies on other, Solaris-specific system components.
key_insight: Open source licensing like the CDDL enables cross-pollination of sophisticated systems software between operating systems, but actually doing the work matters more than talking about it.
